text:
Market today: Mixed but leaning cautious. Micron's blowout and Qualcomm's strong numbers are lifting the chip sector — good tailwind for your tech-heavy book. But geopolitical noise (Iran ops, $87B emergency spend request) keeps macro jittery, which caps how far risk names can run.

Your holdings: PLTR is the sharpest pain — down nearly 13% and flagged SELL; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast. AVGO is your largest position, also down ~12.5% and flagged TRIM — size matters here, trim reduces the bleeding. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record; margin amplifies any sudden drop, so treat it as a live grenade until you have more data.

Watch: (1) Whether the Micron/Qualcomm chip enthusiasm spills into AVGO and SMH tomorrow — that's your clearest near-term lift catalyst. (2) SPAL's decay — leveraged ETFs bleed daily; check it against your stop ($19) aggressively.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers (PLTR, AVGO) both have scanner SELL/TRIM flags — on margin, act on those first before watching for upside.
